project-memory.USER_DIRECTIVES: - Treat Antigravity Claude cache instability primarily as an `opencode-antigravity-auth` plugin issue; use Magic Context changes only for false-BUST reporting cleanup. - Apply fixes incrementally: one fix at a time, build, deploy, restart OpenCode, test in fresh conversation, confirm working before applying next fix. - User chooses which fix to apply next from the presented list — do not apply all fixes at once. - When dispatching delegated tasks, always include `load_skills` and `run_in_background`. - For this cache-bust issue, test plugin-backed Claude models specifically because the user observes the problem with `opencode-antigravity-auth` models and not other providers. - In search-mode, maximize search effort with parallel explore and librarian agents plus direct grep, ripgrep, and ast-grep; never stop at the first result. - In analyze-mode, gather context in parallel before deep work, consult Oracle or Artistry for complex problems, and synthesize findings before proceeding.
project-memory.CONFIG_DEFAULTS: - The old `gemini-3-flash` ("Gemini 3 Flash") still exists as a separate model in the Antigravity API, distinct from Gemini 3.5 Flash. - `gemini-3.1-pro-high` is deprecated in the Antigravity API; `deprecatedModelIds` maps it to `gemini-pro-agent`. - Antigravity API exposes Gemini 3.5 Flash as "High" (`gemini-3-flash-agent`) and "Low" (`gemini-3.5-flash-low`) variants — no "Medium" variant exists; plugin UI labels should use "Low" not "Medium". - `gemini-3-pro-preview` is removed from the plugin and opencode.json — Gemini 3 Pro is fully deprecated and no longer available on Antigravity or Gemini CLI. - `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` is a Gemini CLI-only image generation model (NOT available on Antigravity — returns 404). Added as a gemini-cli model definition in v1.6.14. Requires user's own Google AI API key. - Gemini 3.5 Flash catalog fix (May 20 2026): Gemini CLI screenshot/list exposes `gemini-3-flash-preview` (not `gemini-3.5-flash`), so do not add a user-facing `gemini-3.5-flash` model in OpenCode config. Antigravity exposes Gemini 3.5 Flash High/Medium UI variants; request resolver maps high/default to internal `gemini-3-flash-agent` and medium/low to internal `gemini-3.5-flash-low`. If OpenCode variant providerOptions specifies `thinkingLevel`, `prepareAntigravityRequest()` must recompute `effectiveModel`, not only change `thinkingLevel`, otherwise Medium still sends the High internal model. - Live Antigravity `fetchAvailableModels` (May 20 2026) exposes Gemini 3.5 Flash as `gemini-3-flash-agent` (display `Gemini 3.5 Flash (High)`) and `gemini-3.5-flash-low`; it does NOT expose `gemini-3.5-flash` or `gemini-3.5-flash-medium` as Antigravity model IDs. Gemini CLI quota buckets expose `gemini-3-flash-preview`, not `gemini-3.5-flash`. Resolver should map user-facing `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash` to `gemini-3-flash-agent` and user-facing `gemini-3.5-flash` CLI path to `gemini-3-flash-preview`. - Antigravity Gemini 3.5 Flash resolves with tiered model ids: `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash-high` sends `gemini-3.5-flash-high`, and `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash-medium` sends `gemini-3.5-flash-medium`. - `gemini-3.5-flash` is the stable model name (no `-preview` suffix); the resolver skips appending `-preview` for this model on the gemini-cli path via `isGemini35Flash` check. - `gemini-3-flash-preview` and `gemini-3-pro-preview` remain gemini-cli fallback paths for direct Google API, not Antigravity models. - Antigravity Gemini 3 catalog uses `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash` and `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro`; `antigravity-gemini-3-flash` and `antigravity-gemini-3-pro` are removed. - `gemini-3.5-flash-preview` is the gemini-cli fallback model definition for `gemini-3.5-flash`. - `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking` resolves to `claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking` and supports `low` (8192) and `max` (32768) reasoning variants. - `antigravity-gpt-oss-120b` resolves to `gpt-oss-120b` and supports the `medium` variant. - `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash` resolves to `gemini-3.5-flash`, supports `high` and `medium` variants, and uses a 1,048,576-token context window with 65,536 max output tokens.
project-memory.CONSTRAINTS: - `gemini-2.5-flash-image` and `gemini-2.5-flash-image-preview` require the Google AI API path, not Antigravity OAuth. - Anthropic `thinking` field in message content blocks can be a nested object (from round-tripped conversation history), not just a string. `getThinkingPartText()` in `src/plugin/request.ts` handles both `part.text` (Gemini-style thought) and `part.thinking` (Anthropic-style) for safe string extraction; do not use raw `||` property chains. - The Pydantic error `messages.N.content.M.thinking.thinking: Field required` means the `thinking` TEXT FIELD is MISSING ENTIRELY from a block that matched the "thinking" union variant — NOT that there is a nested `thinking: { thinking: ... }` object. - Never use `.filter()` on `contents` or `parts` arrays in the prompt-cached prefix or system-instruction parts. Use `.map()` with `{ text: "." }` sentinels to preserve array indices and prevent cache hash mismatches. Implemented in `sanitizeRequestPayloadForAntigravity()` in `src/plugin/request.ts` and `stripAllThinkingBlocks()` in `src/plugin/request-helpers.ts`. - `isValidRequestPart()` in `src/plugin/request.ts` allows parts with these keys only: `text`, `functionCall`, `functionResponse`, `inlineData`, `fileData`, `executableCode`, `codeExecutionResult`, `thought`. Parts lacking any of these keys are replaced with `{ text: "." }` sentinels — adding a new valid part type requires updating this allowlist. - When extending `systemInstruction` in `src/plugin/request.ts`, append new `{ text }` parts to the parts array rather than mutating the existing text string, to avoid corrupting cached prefix text. - `JSON.stringify` silently drops keys with `undefined` values. Never assign `thinking: undefined` — always use `thinking: ""` or omit the field entirely. - Sentinel text blocks must contain non-whitespace text; Claude rejects whitespace-only text blocks and the proxy drops empty strings. - Antigravity Claude requests must not include `cache_control` at top-level or part-level because the Google sandbox proxy rejects both as unknown fields. - Sentinel blocks must NOT use thinking/thought format (`{ thought: true }` or `{ type: "thinking" }`) — the Antigravity proxy converts these to Claude thinking blocks and may drop required fields when text is empty.
project-memory.ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS: - Official `google-gemini/gemini-cli` uses `cloudcode-pa.googleapis.com/v1internal` with OAuth client ID `681255809395-...`, broader Google scopes such as `cloud-platform`, wrapped `{ model, project, request }` bodies, and `GeminiCLI/{version}/{model}` user-agent headers. - All plugin models route through the Antigravity proxy with `headerStyle=antigravity` by default. The "Gemini CLI" label in `opencode.json` is cosmetic — `quotaPreference` defaults to `antigravity` unless `cli_first=true` is set in config. The dual header style / dual quota pool design exists in code but is not active by default. `gemini-2.5-flash` works on the Antigravity daily sandbox endpoint despite not being in the `fetchAvailableModels` list. - `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` is not available on Antigravity (404 on all 3 endpoints); removed from plugin in v1.6.17. Only works via direct Google AI API with user's own API key. - `proper-lockfile` is lazy-loaded inside `getLockFunction()` in `src/plugin/storage.ts` (result cached in `_cachedLock` after first call) to avoid ESM/CJS default-export interop failures in the built output. - `deduplicateThinkingText()` in `src/plugin/core/streaming/transformer.ts` omits duplicate thinking chunks (returns `[]` via `flatMap`) instead of emitting blank text blocks, preventing OpenCode from persisting blank assistant messages. - `ensureThinkingFields()` in `src/plugin/request.ts` recursively walks the payload before serialization and adds `thinking: ""` to any `type: "thinking"` block and `text: ""` to any `thought: true` block where those fields are missing or non-string (`typeof !== "string"` catches undefined values that `JSON.stringify` would silently drop). - `stripAllThinkingBlocks()` and `removeTrailingThinkingBlocks()` in `src/plugin/request-helpers.ts` use `.map()` (not `.filter()`) to replace thinking parts with `{ text: ".", cache_control? }` sentinels, preserving array indices for prompt-cache stability. - Plugin intercepts `fetch()` at Phase 3, after oh-my-openagent hooks (Phase 1), magic-context injection (Phase 1), and OpenCode's `applyCaching()` (Phase 2). Any message mutation at Phase 3 risks busting the provider prompt cache. - `claude_tool_hardening` (config: `src/plugin/config/schema.ts`, default: `true`) injects parameter signatures into tool descriptions and adds a system instruction for tool call reliability. Disable via `claude_tool_hardening: false` to reduce context size. - Response-path transformers `transformGeminiCandidate()` and `transformThinkingParts()` in `src/plugin/request-helpers.ts` and `deduplicateThinkingText()` in `src/plugin/core/streaming/transformer.ts` construct output objects with only explicit fields — never `{ ...spread }` — to prevent the original `thinking: <object>` from leaking into output. - `src/plugin/cache/signature-cache.ts` uses sync temp-file + rename pattern (`writeFileSync` → `.tmp`, `renameSync` to target, fallback `writeFileSync` from `readFileSync(tmpPath)`) for atomic cache persistence. - `atomicWriteFile()` in `src/plugin/storage.ts` writes to a hex-suffixed `.tmp` file, retries `fs.rename()` up to 3 times with 50/100/200ms backoff on `EPERM`/`EACCES`, then falls back to `fs.copyFile()` + `fs.unlink()`. Both `saveAccounts()` and `saveAccountsReplace()` use this helper. - `account_selection_strategy` (config: `src/plugin/config/schema.ts`) defaults to `"hybrid"` (health score + token bucket + LRU freshness). Also accepts `"sticky"` (same account until rate-limited; preserves prompt cache) and `"round-robin"` (rotate every request). - OpenCode's `applyCaching()` marks the first 2 system messages and the last 2 non-system messages with `cache_control: { type: "ephemeral" }`. The provider cache hash covers `[tools]+[system]+[messages up to each breakpoint]`. - CLIProxyAPI v6.9.x removed server-side `ANTIGRAVITY_SYSTEM_INSTRUCTION` injection. The plugin does not inject it either. - `cache_control` markers exist at two levels in the transform pipeline: part-level (preserved through all transforms) and nested-inside-content (stripped when content is extracted to a string). Part-level markers are OpenCode's caching breakpoints and must survive the transform pipeline. - Magic context's `tags` table in `context.db` uses a SQLite trigger to upsert duplicate `(session_id, tag_number)` inserts because the native addon issues plain inserts. - `src/plugin/request.ts` defines `SENTINEL_SIGNATURE = "skip_thought_signature_validator"` (distinct from the imported `SKIP_THOUGHT_SIGNATURE` constant). On the thinking-signature restoration path, `ensureThoughtSignature()` injects `SENTINEL_SIGNATURE` into `thoughtSignature`/`signature` fields of thinking parts that lack a valid signature. - `signatureCache` prunes expired session maps and caps the outer session map at 10 sessions to avoid one-entry-per-session RAM growth. - OpenCode v1.14.41 internal Codex OAuth plugin returns non-empty `OAUTH_DUMMY_KEY = "opencode-oauth-dummy-key"` from auth loader (`apiKey: OAUTH_DUMMY_KEY`) before replacing auth headers. opencode-antigravity-auth currently returns `apiKey: ""`, which can trip Desktop/@ai-sdk provider initialization before fetch interception. - Claude models (`isClaudeModel()` in `src/plugin/request.ts`) reject assistant message prefill. When `contents` or `messages` ends on a model/assistant turn, the plugin appends `{ role: "user", parts: [{ text: "[Continue]" }] }` to both array paths. - `sanitizeThinkingInMessages()` in `src/plugin/request.ts` wraps `deepSanitizeThinkingFields()` to scope recursion to `contents`, `messages`, `system`, and `systemInstruction` only — never `tools`/`function_declarations` — because tool schemas can legitimately contain `thinking` properties (e.g., `google_search`'s `thinking: {type: "boolean"}`). Both `JSON.stringify` call sites use the scoped wrapper. - The Antigravity plugin activates its fetch interceptor path only when OpenCode's `getAuth()` returns an OAuth credential (`isOAuthAuth(auth)` — checks `type === "oauth"`). If no valid OAuth auth exists, the loader clears stale account storage and returns `{}`, bypassing the intercept path entirely. - `safeStringify()` in `request.ts` wraps `JSON.stringify` with a replacer function that flattens any non-string `thinking` field to `""` at serialization time, while preserving legitimate Schema objects in `tools`/`function_declarations` (detected by `JSON_SCHEMA_TYPES` set containing standard JSON Schema types). - Claude request sanitization in `src/plugin/request.ts` rewrites empty or whitespace-only Anthropic `messages[].content[].text` blocks to `"."` before serialization so Claude accepts the request. - Thinking-replacement sentinels use `{ text: "." }` with inherited `cache_control` for two reasons: (1) empty text is dropped by the Antigravity proxy and whitespace-only text is rejected by Claude; (2) OpenCode persists transformed responses and replays them into later request history, so sentinels must survive the round-trip as valid content. - OpenCode activates the Antigravity Google auth/fetch path only when `auth.json` contains a stored `google` OAuth entry; without it, native `@ai-sdk/google` initializes and requires an API key. - Desktop OpenCode v1.14.41 utility-process server uses stricter Node/Electron ESM than TUI/Bun. The package failed to load because dist/index.js re-exported extensionless ./src/plugin. Fix in @expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.3 bundles index.ts with esbuild after tsc, bundling internal source and @opencode-ai/plugin while leaving @openauthjs/*, proper-lockfile, and zod external. Verify with node import('./dist/index.js'). - Build runs `tsc -p tsconfig.build.json` then `esbuild index.ts --bundle --platform=node --format=esm --outfile=dist/index.js`. Internal source and `@opencode-ai/plugin` are bundled; `@openauthjs/*`, `proper-lockfile`, and `zod` are external. This produces a single bundled entrypoint that works under Desktop/Electron strict ESM.
project-memory.ENVIRONMENT: - `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` source and published npm version is `1.6.17`. - `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\opencode.json` contains the 14-model Antigravity/Gemini CLI catalog (6 Antigravity + 8 Gemini CLI) after `gemini-3-pro-preview` removal in v1.6.15. - `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\opencode.json` contains the 15-model Antigravity/Gemini CLI catalog, including the newly added `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` gemini-cli model. - OpenCode config loads `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@latest`. - The active OpenCode package cache contains an unpublished local Gemini 3.5 Flash resolver fix that must be user-tested before npm publishing. - Magic context log file: `C:\Users\Expiren\AppData\Local\Temp\magic-context.log`. - Magic context SQLite database: `C:\Users\Expiren\.local\share\opencode\storage\plugin\magic-context\context.db`. - Magic Context user config path: `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\magic-context.jsonc`. - `proper-lockfile` v4.1.2 is CJS; exports both `module.exports = lock` and `module.exports.lock = lock`. - npm is logged in as `expiren`; publishing requires an npm automation or granular access token with bypass-2FA enabled. - Local workspace: `D:\Projects\opencode-antigravity-auth`. - Antigravity user config path: `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\antigravity.json`. - Build output lands in `dist/src/` (mirrors `src/` structure). - Local installed Magic Context copies in the OpenCode package cache and config `node_modules` contain a manual `message.updated.no_usage` patch for usage-less zero-token `message.updated` events. - `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\antigravity.json` sets `claude_tool_hardening=false`. - `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\opencode.json` contains the 14-model Antigravity/Gemini CLI catalog, including `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash`, `gemini-3.5-flash-preview`, `antigravity-gemini-3.1-flash-image`, `gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview`, `antigravity-gpt-oss-120b`, and `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking`. - `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` source is bumped to `v1.6.6` and pushed to `origin/main`; npm publishing for `v1.6.6` requires a granular npm token with bypass-2FA automation permission. - Local workspace: `D:\Projects\opencode-antigravity-auth` - GitHub repo `https://github.com/expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` is the active origin fork containing the `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` scoped-package changes and upstream NoeFabris merges. - Desktop/Electron strict Node ESM rejects extensionless relative imports (e.g., `./src/plugin`); TUI/Bun tolerates them.
project-memory.WORKFLOW_RULES: - For Claude cache-stability experiments, run controlled A/B tests: baseline current behavior, then `claude_tool_hardening=false`, then sticky/cache-first account settings. - Deploy: `npm run build` then `npm publish --access public` publishes `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` to npm. OpenCode loads it from `C:\Users\Expiren\.cache\opencode\packages\@expiren\` via `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@latest`. - When debugging persistent issues across many fix iterations, reset to a known-working baseline and apply fixes one at a time with user testing between each.
project-memory.KNOWN_ISSUES: - The plugin's gemini-cli path routes to the Antigravity proxy (`cloudcode-pa.googleapis.com`) with nodejs-client UA headers, NOT to the public Google API — gemini-cli models may only work if the proxy recognizes their model IDs. Manual `/v1internal` API testing returns 401 even for known-working models, so live OpenCode testing is the only reliable verification method. - OpenCode Desktop may not expose plugin OAuth methods for Google and can fall back to the built-in Google API-key connect screen even when TUI shows the Antigravity OAuth method. - Antigravity Claude prompt-cache markers are unsupported on the current proxy path; enabling Claude auto-caching causes invalid JSON payload 400s. - OpenCode Desktop v1.14.41 can fall back to native `@ai-sdk/google` even when `auth.json` contains `google` OAuth and TUI works, causing `Google Generative AI API key is missing`. - Magic Context guidance injection (`hephaestus`, `sisyphus`, `sisyphus-junior`, or generic guidance) can change the system prompt hash and cause cache busts even after the v0.14.1 sentinel fix. - Account rotation can split provider cache buckets by changing auth identity, managed project, fingerprint, or headers; sticky account selection improves prompt-cache stability when quota allows. - If magic context recreates `context.db`, the duplicate-tag upsert trigger must be re-added. - Desktop cannot use the local plugin fork — the plugin fetch interceptor does not activate for Desktop's request path regardless of dummy-key or local-wrapper approaches, causing "API key not valid" errors. - The Antigravity account manager lacks a "Use this account" restore action, so existing stored accounts cannot recreate missing OpenCode `google` OAuth auth without re-running OAuth. - Upstream npm package `opencode-antigravity-auth` is archived with unfixed bugs; OpenCode Desktop resolves and caches it separately from local builds. - Conversations with persisted `thinking: <object>` history keep failing with `messages.*.content.*.thinking.thinking: Field required`; test fixes in a fresh conversation after restarting OpenCode. - Local `opencode run` may fail before model execution with a stale `Session not found` reference to `ses_2a1a871c1ffecZaQ5XpN53G1a1`; use a fresh OpenCode TUI session for live Claude validation if that occurs. - Plugin-backed Claude models are more cache-fragile than other providers because Claude tool hardening, interleaved-thinking hints, and tool-description signature injection can change the prompt prefix. - Magic context Phase 1 stripping, placeholder replay, and compartment-agent activity can cause alternating cache misses even when the plugin request pipeline is stable. - Magic Context plugin counts `message.updated` events with `hasUsageTokens=false` as cache BUSTs, inflating BUST diagnostics for Antigravity Claude models — fix needed in Magic Context to ignore zero-usage events. - `appendClaudeThinkingHint()` in `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` mutates the last system text part, destabilizing Claude prefix cache — should append a separate `{ text: hint }` part instead. - Antigravity API rejects explicit Claude `cache_control` markers, so normal Anthropic cache breakpoints are unavailable. - Claude thinking-hint injection has two call sites, `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` and `src/plugin/request.ts`; verify whether both fire on the same Claude thinking request before consolidating to one implementation. - `gemini-3.5-flash-preview` is missing from `OPENCODE_MODEL_DEFINITIONS`, so Gemini 3.5 Flash lacks a defined gemini-cli preview path even though the resolver can generate that fallback id. - TypeScript LSP diagnostics can fail with server exit `-4058`; use `npm run typecheck` as the reliable TypeScript validation path. - `gemini-3.1-flash-image` and `gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview` are available on Gemini API, Vertex AI, and Antigravity with image output, but the plugin lacks explicit `OPENCODE_MODEL_DEFINITIONS` entries for them. - Antigravity API rejects explicit Claude `cache_control` markers, so normal Anthropic cache breakpoints are unavailable - `appendClaudeThinkingHint()` in `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` mutates the last system text part, destabilizing Claude prefix cache — should append a separate `{ text: hint }` part instead - Magic Context plugin counts `message.updated` events with `hasUsageTokens=false` as cache BUSTs, inflating BUST diagnostics for Antigravity Claude models — fix needed in Magic Context to ignore zero-usage events - `saveAccounts()` and `saveAccountsReplace()` in `storage.ts` lack retry/fallback on `fs.rename()`, causing `EPERM` on Windows when another process locks the file during atomic rename - Account state persistence (`executeSave()` in `accounts.ts`) can transiently fail with EPERM on Windows during atomic `.tmp` → `antigravity-accounts.json` rename when another process holds a file lock. Non-crashing — next save usually succeeds. No retry mechanism implemented yet. - Thinking sentinel objects (`{type:"thinking", thinking:""}` and Gemini-format `{thought:true, text:"", thoughtSignature:SENTINEL_SIGNATURE}`) cause HTTP 400 "Unknown name" errors from the Antigravity API for fields `type`, `thinking`, and `signature`. Sentinels must be stripped or converted to API-compatible empty-text parts before the final request is sent to `generativelanguage.googleapis.com`. - OpenCode `providerOptions`/`cache_control` markers may be consumed before the fetch body, so the Antigravity proxy can receive no explicit cache-control markers from OpenCode. - Latest OpenCode versions may cause `/provider` and `/config/providers` to return 400 when the Antigravity plugin is loaded (GitHub issues #26546, #26547). Adding provider-model metadata fields (`id`, `release_date`, `attachment`, `reasoning`, `temperature`, `tool_call`) is required — the plugin is currently in active config with these fields populated in all model definitions. - OpenCode Desktop v1.14.41 can fall back to native `@ai-sdk/google` even when `auth.json` contains `google` OAuth and TUI works, causing `Google Generative AI API key is missing`; the likely failure boundary is Desktop's utility-process server dropping or bypassing the plugin auth-loader `fetch` override.

compartment.title: Rebuilt cache and thinking fixes from baseline

compartment: Cloned `https://github.com/NoeFabris/opencode-antigravity-auth.git` under `D:\Projects\opencode-antigravity-auth`, preserved old logs, fixed `proper-lockfile` CJS/ESM interop in `src/plugin/storage.ts`, and investigated Claude thinking/cache failures through several sanitizer and serialization attempts. After over-broad thinking stripping corrupted tool schemas, reset twice to upstream archive baseline, reapplied only proven fixes incrementally, and rebuilt cache-control preservation with scoped recursive stripping, `cachedContent`/`cached_content` normalization, marker preservation, index-stable sentinels, clean build, 914/914 tests, and deployment to both installs. U: best solution is to remove all files of that project and start from 0 and after each modification we did we will test workflow pivot became incremental testing from known-good baseline instead of stacking speculative fixes. This changed the workflow from stacking speculative patches to validating each change against the original known-good source. U: use this version as base is @opencode-antigravity-auth.7z i mean is original archive became source-of-truth baseline for future changes. `@opencode-antigravity-auth.7z` became the reset baseline for the later cache/thinking fixes.

compartment.title: Replaced invalid sentinels and diagnosed Magic Context

compartment: Reapplied missed sanitizer work, added `safeStringify()` at serialization sites, and found Gemini-format sentinels were being converted by Antigravity into invalid Claude thinking blocks. Changed sentinel creation sites in `request.ts`, `request-helpers.ts`, and `thinking-recovery.ts` from Gemini sentinels to plain text placeholders, eventually stabilizing on request/response-safe non-whitespace sentinels while Magic Context diagnostics showed stable plugin payload hashes but cache misses tied to Magic Context transformations; SQLite gaps and duplicate tags were repaired, artifacts removed, and tests/build/deploy passed.

compartment.title: Committed cache audit and assistant continuation guard

compartment: Committed accumulated cache/sentinel fixes and app bug-audit fixes as `f7744bf`, then fixed `signatureCache` session map pruning/cap in `a7a6f3d` and broader error-handling, entropy, debug-redaction, SSE, queued-save, and `.gitignore` logging issues in `f23ae74`. A deep cache audit found the last cache-bust vector in `systemInstruction.parts.filter(isValidRequestPart)`, switched it to sentinel-preserving `.map`, added 5-minute `projectContextResultCache` TTL, committed `a1a08c5`, then fixed post-compaction Claude assistant-prefill rejection by appending synthetic `[Continue]` user messages and committed `4d456e4`.

compartment.title: Filed Magic Context issues and fixed Claude empty text

compartment: Investigated remaining Magic Context cache instability, identified sentinel replacement as the fix for structural `.filter()`/`.splice()` stripping paths, opened `cortexkit/opencode-magic-context#35`, submitted doctor diagnostics as `#37`, and redeployed `opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.0` after typecheck, 914 tests, and build. Also fixed Claude 400s from empty/whitespace request text blocks by rewriting Anthropic `messages[]` text to `"."`, then changed response-only placeholders in `src/plugin/core/streaming/transformer.ts` back to `""` to stop visible `.OK` leaks while preserving request-path `"."`.

compartment.title: Removed rejected cache markers and traced plugin-specific cache churn

compartment: Removed invalid top-level `request.cache_control` and rejected part-level `request.contents[].parts[].cache_control` injection after Antigravity 400s, verified `src/plugin/request.test.ts`, typecheck, deploy, and absence of cache-control injection. Later cache analysis concluded account rotation can split provider cache buckets, sticky account mode is best for cache-sensitive Claude sessions, partial prefix-cache hits are normal, Magic Context/system-prompt churn contributes to busts, but plugin-backed Claude models are especially sensitive because Antigravity rejects explicit `cache_control`; set `execute_threshold_tokens` to `120000` for default and both Antigravity Claude models in `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\magic-context.jsonc`. U: you must note this issue only happened with our plugin models not with others. That correction shifted diagnosis toward `opencode-antigravity-auth` Claude-only mutations: interleaved-thinking hints, tool hardening instructions, tool-description signature injection, and possible account-rotation cache bucket changes. The diagnosis broadened from Magic Context alone to plugin-amplified Claude cache churn.

compartment.title: Fixed blank Claude streams and tried Desktop dummy key

compartment: Fixed completed Claude streams that showed thinking but blank visible assistant output by omitting empty duplicate thinking chunks from `deduplicateThinkingText` in `src/plugin/core/streaming/transformer.ts`, added regression coverage, verified focused tests, `src/plugin/request.test.ts` with 110 passing tests, typecheck/build, and deployed to `opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.0`. Then investigated Desktop `Google Generative AI API key is missing`, found Desktop initialized native `@ai-sdk/google` before fetch interception, tried matching Codex OAuth with `apiKey: "antigravity-oauth-dummy-key"` in `src/plugin.ts`, verified and deployed the dummy-key build.

compartment.title: Tried config injection and reverted dummy-key changes

compartment: Because Desktop still initialized `@ai-sdk/google` from `opencode.json` before plugin auth loader, `src/plugin/config/updater.ts` was changed to inject `provider.google.options.apiKey = "antigravity-oauth-dummy-key"` when missing/empty, and a local wrapper re-exported `D:/Projects/opencode-antigravity-auth/dist/index.js`. Invalid-key errors proved the dummy key reached real Google because Desktop fetch interception was inactive, so all dummy-key and wrapper changes were reverted, config restored to `opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.0`, tests/typecheck/build passed, reverted dist was redeployed, and stale dummy-key memory was deleted. U: revert modification you did it cause same issue with tui "API key not valid. pass valid API key." only use gpt model. The rollback restored `src/plugin.ts` to `apiKey: ""`, removed updater logic/tests and `provider.google.options.apiKey`, deleted the wrapper, and avoided dummy keys.

compartment.title: Published scoped fork and merged upstream

compartment: Renamed the package to `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth`, updated config updater and auto-update checker constants/tests, fixed a pre-existing `request-helpers.test.ts` expectation, passed all 917 tests, committed, pushed to `expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth`, published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.0`, and updated `opencode.json` to `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@latest`. Then merged 35 upstream commits from `NoeFabris/main`, resolved `src/plugin/fingerprint.ts` by keeping both fork `crypto.getRandomValues` entropy fix and upstream `platformToDisplayName()`, updated checker tests, verified all 1000 tests passed, and pushed the merge.

compartment.title: Published 1.6.1 and 1.6.2 auth-state guard

compartment: Published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.1` after npm rejected republishing `1.6.0`, committed the version bump as `02ff3d7`, and pushed it. Then separated the TUI/Desktop mismatch from the `undefined is not an object (evaluating 'auth.type')` crash, made `isOAuthAuth()` in `src/plugin/auth.ts` null/undefined-safe, added regression coverage, verified auth tests/typecheck/build, published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.2`, and committed `708d7c5`; untracked `.sisyphus/`, `magic-context-issue--.md`, and `opencode-antigravity-auth.7z` were left untouched.

compartment.title: Diagnosed missing Google auth state and Desktop fallback

compartment: Investigated OpenCode diagnostics, config, cached source, provider loader behavior, auth state, package contents, GitHub issues, and release notes. Determined OpenCode uses `opencode debug info/config/paths/startup`, the installed `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.2` package contained the `auth.type` guard, but `auth.json` lacked a stored `google` OAuth entry so OpenCode skipped the plugin `auth.loader` and fell through to native `@ai-sdk/google` with `apiKey: ""`; recommended `opencode auth login -p google -m "OAuth with Google (Antigravity)"`, checking `opencode auth list`, restarting, and avoiding dummy keys. After the user confirmed TUI showed `Google oauth`, the remaining failure was narrowed to Desktop using separate state and hitting native Google initialization before the plugin OAuth/fetch override, while TUI worked.

compartment.title: Researched Desktop utility-process regression and added debug instrumentation

compartment: Corrected the prior issue mapping: `anomalyco/opencode#21008` was the old plugin-path problem affecting both TUI and Desktop, while the current failure is a new Desktop-only regression. Researched OpenCode Desktop v1.14.41 release/source changes, ruled out `.well-known/opencode` remote config because none existed locally and it would affect both runtimes, and identified `feat(desktop): move server to utilityProcess` / PR `#25962` as the likely suspect because Desktop now forks a sidecar server process that may drop or bypass plugin auth-loader options such as custom `fetch` while TUI still applies them. Added temporary debug instrumentation to `src/plugin.ts` and deployed it into the active cached `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@latest` package. It logs plugin initialization, `auth.loader` start/return, stored auth/OAuth/account-storage status, `fetch override invoked`, and latest auth seen inside fetch, without logging tokens, headers, prompts, request bodies, emails, or full URLs. Verified diagnostics, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, cache deployment to `C:\Users\Expiren\.cache\opencode\packages\@expiren\opencode-antigravity-auth@latest\node_modules\@expiren\opencode-antigravity-auth\dist`, and SHA256 match. The diagnostic interpretation was: `auth.loader returning fetch override` without `fetch override invoked` means Desktop bypasses/drops `options.fetch`; no `auth.loader started` means Desktop is not running the plugin auth loader for `google`.

compartment.title: Fixed and published Desktop strict ESM bundle

compartment: Diagnosed OpenCode Desktop failing to load the plugin: TUI logs showed `[antigravity-debug]`, but Desktop logged `Directory import '...\dist\src\plugin' is not supported resolving ES modules` and `failed to load plugin`, causing fallback "Google Generative AI API key is missing" behavior. Root cause was `tsc` output in `dist/index.js` using extensionless relative imports such as `./src/plugin`, tolerated by Bun/TUI but rejected by Desktop/Node strict ESM. Implemented esbuild bundling by changing `package.json` build to run `tsc -p tsconfig.build.json` then `esbuild index.ts --bundle --platform=node --format=esm --outfile=dist/index.js`; switched from `--packages=external` to explicitly externalizing `@openauthjs/*`, `proper-lockfile`, and `zod` while bundling `@opencode-ai/plugin` inline. Added `esbuild`, verified typecheck, 1001 tests, build, strict `node -e "import('./dist/index.js')"`, cache deployment, and SHA256 match. Removed the temporary `[antigravity-debug]` instrumentation, bumped version to 1.6.3, and re-ran typecheck, full tests (1001 passed), build, and strict Node import verification. Committed `fix: bundle package entry for desktop` as `d9361f2`, pushed to GitHub, and published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.3` to npm. Confirmed OpenCode config still references `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@latest` and stored project memory for the esbuild bundling architecture.

compartment.title: Investigated and published OpenCode v1.14.43 compatibility hardening

compartment: Investigated a new OpenCode startup failure where enabling the plugin made `/provider` and `/config/providers` return 400 with `tui bootstrap failed`, referencing GitHub issues #26546 and #26547; logs also showed a separate stale-config `path=update error=Plugin export is not a function`. OpenCode provider source review indicated tightened provider-model validation, so `src/plugin/config/models.ts` was patched to add required metadata (`id`, `release_date`, `attachment`, `reasoning`, `temperature`, `tool_call`), regression tests were added in `updater.test.ts`, local `opencode.json` model shapes were updated, and verification passed with 1002 tests, build, and deploy. The user confirmed this metadata fix did not resolve the 400 startup failure, so release was paused; the plugin was removed from active config as a workaround, background research timed out, and the true OpenCode compatibility root cause remained unidentified at this point. Confirmed the startup failure was an OpenCode v1.14.42 bug fixed by upgrading to v1.14.43: auth loaders adding non-JSON options such as the plugin’s custom `fetch` function to providers caused 400 responses from `/provider` and `/config/providers`. Kept the provider model metadata improvements as valid hardening. Commit `2cfab08` was pushed to GitHub and `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.4` was published to npm.

compartment.title: Implemented and published EPERM-safe atomic account persistence

compartment: Investigated Windows `EPERM: operation not permitted` when renaming `antigravity-accounts.json.*.tmp` to `antigravity-accounts.json`, traced to single `fs.rename()` calls in `saveAccounts()` and `saveAccountsReplace()` in `src/plugin/storage.ts` after tmp-file writes. Root cause was transient Windows locks from antivirus/indexers/watchers during atomic rename; the plugin continued running and later saves usually succeeded. Extracted a shared `atomicWriteFile` helper with rename retry/backoff (3 attempts at 50ms/100ms/200ms) and copy+delete fallback, matching the existing `signature-cache.ts` pattern, then applied it to both account save paths. Typecheck, storage tests, and persist account pool tests passed. Completed full verification of the `atomicWriteFile` account persistence fix: typecheck clean, 1002/1002 tests passed, build clean, and active package cache deploy verified by hash. Committed `9db04e7` with message `fix: add retry with backoff for atomic file rename on Windows`, bumped version from 1.6.4 to 1.6.5, pushed to GitHub, and published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.5` to npm.

compartment.title: Diagnosed Claude cache instability and patched Magic Context false-BUSTs

compartment: Investigated repeated Magic Context BUST diagnostics when using Claude models through `opencode-antigravity-auth`. Found two issues: Magic Context falsely classified usage-less zero-token `message.updated` events (`hasUsageTokens=false`) as cache BUSTs, while real cache instability likely came from the plugin’s Claude transform path. Plugin risks identified were `appendClaudeThinkingHint()` mutating the last existing system text part instead of appending a separate part, `claude_tool_hardening` defaulting to true and injecting tool signature/system text, and account rotation splitting cache buckets despite active `account_selection_strategy=sticky`. Because Antigravity rejects explicit Claude `cache_control` markers, the working priority became fixing Magic Context reporting separately while treating actual cache instability as primarily a plugin issue. Patched both installed Magic Context package copies so usage-less zero-token `message.updated` events log as `message.updated.no_usage` than normal usage events: `C:\Users\Expiren\.cache\opencode\packages\@cortexkit\opencode-magic-context@latest\...\dist\index.js` and `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\node_modules\@cortexkit\opencode-magic-context\dist\index.js`. Verified both patched files contain `message.updated.no_usage` and pass `node --check`. Then reviewed plugin-side cache-risk points and recommended the lowest-risk next fix: change `appendClaudeThinkingHint()` in `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` to stop mutating existing system text, while deferring `claude_tool_hardening` testing and temporary cache identity hashes until after a fresh restart/test.

compartment.title: Applied Claude thinking hint cache fix and validation plan

compartment: Applied Fix 1 in `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts`: `appendClaudeThinkingHint()` now preserves existing string/object system instructions and appends the Claude interleaved-thinking hint as a separate `{ text: hint }` system part instead of concatenating onto existing system text. Updated `src/plugin/transform/claude.test.ts`, confirmed a red regression first, then verified focused Claude transform tests (67/67), full tests (1002/1002), `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, strict ESM import, and active cache deployment with hash verification for `dist/index.js` and `dist/src/plugin/transform/claude.js`. The user correction about `"claude_tool_hardening": false` was acknowledged: the setting is not present in config, but the code option exists and defaults to `true`; it was not changed. A manual validation plan was produced: restart OpenCode, clear/timestamp `C:\Users\Expiren\AppData\Local\Temp\magic-context.log`, start a fresh plugin-Claude conversation, send 3–5 similar prompts without switching models/accounts, and check for stable/increasing `cache.read`, no repeated system prompt hash changes, and `message.updated.no_usage` instead of false BUSTs; if cache still busts, add temporary hashes for system, tools, selected account, managed project, and fingerprint/user-agent.

compartment.title: Verified sentinels and audited Claude cache-busting vectors

compartment: Investigated whether plugin `{ text: "." }` sentinel parts appear in visible Claude responses. Found sentinel creation sites are request-path only and go to the API as conversation history to preserve array indices for cache stability, while response-path code (`transformGeminiCandidate`, `transformThinkingParts`, `deduplicateThinkingText`) emits clean objects with no `"."` text. The dots in the screenshot were OpenCode UI separators for background explore agent tool calls, not plugin sentinels; no fix was needed. Ran a comprehensive cache-bust audit across this fork, upstream NoeFabris, and alternatives Draculabo/AntigravityManager and lbjlaq/Antigravity-Manager. The fork was better than upstream on cache vectors: upstream deletes with `.filter()`, mutates system text, strips all `cache_control`, leaks thinking objects through spread, and casts objects through `as string`; alternatives are server-side proxies with clean mapping but no cache-control handling. Remaining plugin risks were ranked: HIGH normal-path risks from `claude_tool_hardening` system/tool mutations and `[Continue]` injection, MEDIUM recovery-path risks from `.filter()`/`.splice()` mutations, while sentinel transforms were safe. Magic Context analysis showed `message.updated.no_usage` working, Phase 1 strip counts varying, stable system hashes when config is stable, and compaction potentially triggering trailing assistant turns and `[Continue]`; recommended testing `claude_tool_hardening=false`, consolidating thinking-hint injection, and tracking Magic Context cache effects separately.

compartment.title: Consolidated Claude thinking hint injection

compartment: Checked the active plugin config after the user challenged the `claude_tool_hardening=false` experiment and confirmed `claude_tool_hardening` is supported, defaults to `true`, but is already set to `false` in `antigravity.json`, ruling it out as the live cache-bust cause. Then investigated suspected duplicate Claude thinking-hint injection and found `applyClaudeTransforms()` in `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` is test-only, with the only production injection path in `src/plugin/request.ts` around lines 1196-1218. Remaining likely cache churn sources were Magic Context Phase 1 changes, `[Continue]` insertion after compaction, or other external prompt mutations. Refactored Claude thinking-hint handling so `src/plugin/request.ts` imports and calls `appendClaudeThinkingHint(requestPayload)` instead of maintaining a separate inline injection block. Updated `appendClaudeThinkingHint()` in `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts` to append with a new array (`[...partsValue, { text: hint }]`) than mutating shared `parts` arrays in place. Verified focused Claude transform tests (67/67), request tests (110/110), full suite (1002/1002), typecheck, build, active package-cache deployment, and deployed hash match; committed `100ca457`.

compartment.title: Provided Claude cache validation prompts

compartment: Manual validation still required restarting OpenCode and sending requests through the local plugin, so a fresh-conversation test plan was provided. It used `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking`, five Fibonacci prompts, a PowerShell `Select-String` command against `C:\Users\Expiren\AppData\Local\Temp\magic-context.log`, and pass/fail criteria for cache hits, system-prompt hash stability, and `message.updated.no_usage` classification.

compartment.title: Committed and published v1.6.6

compartment: Repository status showed uncommitted changes in `src/plugin/request.ts`, `src/plugin/transform/claude.ts`, `src/plugin/transform/claude.test.ts`, and `package-lock.json`, covering `appendClaudeThinkingHint()`, shared-array mutation avoidance, tests, and a lockfile update. The work was committed as `96b56f0`, package version bumped to `v1.6.6`, and pushed to `origin/main`. `npm publish --access public` remained blocked: npm auth was fixed enough for `npm whoami` to succeed as `expiren`, but the token lacks bypass-2FA automation permission, so the next step is generating a granular npm token for `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth` with “Bypass 2FA for automation” enabled and rerunning the publish. Configured npm auth with the user-provided token and published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.6`. Committed, pushed, and advised restarting OpenCode before rerunning cache test prompts.

compartment.title: Added missing Antigravity models and published v1.6.7

compartment: Compared the model catalog to the Antigravity screenshot and fixed three gaps: Gemini 3.5 Flash variants, Claude Sonnet 4.6 thinking support, and GPT-OSS 120B. Updated model definitions, resolver aliases, and tests; removed the old non-thinking Claude Sonnet 4.6 entry; verified all 1002 tests passed; published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.7`; committed and pushed `9814b41`.

compartment.title: Verified Gemini 3.5 Flash token limits

compartment: Checked the Gemini 3.5 Flash limits in `src/plugin/config/models.ts` against the user-provided Antigravity spec. Confirmed the plugin already defines `contextWindow=1048576` and `maxOutputTokens=65536`, matching the 1M context window and 64K output limit, so no code change was needed.

compartment.title: Added Gemini 3.5 Flash gemini-cli fallback

compartment: Investigated whether `Gemini 3.5 Flash` exists on the gemini-cli path rather than only the Antigravity path. Found that the fallback resolver would generate `gemini-3.5-flash-preview`, but `OPENCODE_MODEL_DEFINITIONS` does not define that preview model, unlike the existing `gemini-3-flash-preview`, `gemini-3-pro-preview`, and `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` entries. Researched whether Gemini 3.5 Flash is available through Gemini CLI or only Antigravity and found it is publicly available, including on Gemini CLI, not Antigravity-internal only. Added `gemini-3.5-flash-preview` as the gemini-cli fallback model definition, updated model tests, verified the build/tests, bumped and published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.8`, then committed and pushed the release.

compartment.title: Checked Gemini image model availability

compartment: Investigated the listed Gemini image models against the plugin's model resolver/config comments. `gemini-2.5-flash-image` and its preview variant are explicitly noted as unsupported by Antigravity and available only through the Google AI API, while `Gemini 3.1 Flash Image (Preview)` is not mentioned in the plugin code; none of the three listed image models are currently defined by the plugin, so users need OpenCode's native `@ai-sdk/google` provider with their own API key for those Google AI API-only image models. Researched `Gemini 3.1 Flash Image` against public Gemini API, Vertex AI, Gemini CLI context, and Antigravity availability. Found that `gemini-3.1-flash-image` and `gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview` exist, support image generation/editing, and are available on both the public Gemini API and Antigravity; the plugin’s image-routing regex already catches them, but `OPENCODE_MODEL_DEFINITIONS` has no explicit model entries for either id. Also confirmed the earlier 2.5 Flash Image models remain Google AI API-only for this plugin because they are not Antigravity OAuth models.

compartment.title: Added Gemini 3.1 Flash Image and published v1.6.9

compartment: Added explicit `gemini-3.1-flash-image` model definitions for both Antigravity and gemini-cli paths, updated the resolver alias/comment, and adjusted model tests for the expanded catalog. Verification passed with clean typecheck, focused model config/resolver tests, and build; the package was bumped and published as `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.9`, then committed as `e3e79fe` and pushed to GitHub.

compartment.title: Checked obsolete Gemini 3 model references

compartment: Checked the suspected bare Gemini 3 Antigravity models and found the local config files did not reference them, but the plugin still defines `antigravity-gemini-3-pro` and `antigravity-gemini-3-flash`. Because the Antigravity catalog could not be queried directly, removal and `opencode.json` / `oh-my-openagent.json` updates were paused pending user confirmation from the Antigravity dashboard/UI.

compartment.title: Removed obsolete Gemini 3 Antigravity models and published v1.6.10

compartment: Confirmed from Antigravity that `gemini-3-flash` and `gemini-3-pro` are no longer available, with only Gemini 3.5 Flash and Gemini 3.1 Pro remaining. Removed `antigravity-gemini-3-flash` and `antigravity-gemini-3-pro` from the plugin model catalog and resolver aliases, updated `opencode.json`, replaced seven `antigravity-gemini-3-flash` references in `oh-my-openagent.json` with `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash`, and switched the Magic Context sidekick model in `magic-context.jsonc` to `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash`. Kept the direct Google API gemini-cli fallback models `gemini-3-flash-preview` and `gemini-3-pro-preview` intact, verified 1002/1002 tests plus build, then committed `e0a4fd8`, pushed, and published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.10`.

compartment.title: Validated and fixed Gemini 3.5 Flash tier resolution

compartment: Updated the stale e2e model test catalog and attempted the full model validation run across 13 current models. All CLI-driven checks failed before reaching provider execution with the known local `opencode run` stale `Session not found` bug, so manual TUI validation was required instead. Investigated why only Antigravity Gemini 3.5 Flash failed while other models worked, and traced it to `src/plugin/transform/model-resolver.ts` stripping `high`/`medium` tiers from Flash while Antigravity requires tiered model ids. Updated `resolveModelWithTier`, `resolveModelForHeaderStyle`, and `resolveModelWithVariant` so Flash keeps tier suffixes like Pro, updated resolver/request tests, verified the full suite and build, then bumped and published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.11` with commit `d8ffcf8`.

compartment.title: Fixed missing model entries in OpenCode config

compartment: Investigated why Gemini 3.5 Flash appeared without Antigravity/Gemini CLI labeling and found `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\opencode.json` was missing several model definitions even though the plugin code already defined them. Added the missing Antigravity and gemini-cli entries, including `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash` and `gemini-3.5-flash-preview`, fixed `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6` to `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking`, and verified the config JSON now contains all 14 expected models.

compartment.title: Instrumented Gemini 3.5 Flash failures

compartment: Investigated why both `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash` and the `gemini-3.5-flash` CLI path still failed after the resolver/config fixes. Traced the resolver, request path, model definitions, plugin entrypoint, docs, and external references, but found no reliable public mapping for the new model ID; deployed a temporary debug build to the active package cache to log `[antigravity-debug]` details including `effectiveModel`, `requestedModel`, `headerStyle`, HTTP status, and API error body. The next step is for the user to restart OpenCode, retry Gemini 3.5 Flash, and provide the debug line so the expected Antigravity API model ID can be identified.

compartment.title: Published Gemini 3.5 Flash stable-name fix

compartment: Researched Gemini 3.5 Flash public model naming and concluded the Gemini CLI path was incorrectly generating `gemini-3.5-flash-preview`; changed the catalog/resolver/config to use `gemini-3.5-flash` without `-preview`, removed temporary debug instrumentation from `src/plugin.ts`, verified tests/typecheck/build, deployed the clean build to the active package cache, bumped the package to `1.6.12`, pushed, and published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.12` to npm. The user was instructed to restart OpenCode and test both `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash` and `gemini-3.5-flash`.

compartment.title: Applied unpublished base-model Gemini 3.5 Flash resolver fix

compartment: After the user reported Gemini 3.5 Flash still failed silently on both CLI and Antigravity paths, researched public docs and code, then changed `src/plugin/transform/model-resolver.ts` so all Gemini 3.5 Flash variants use the base API model id `gemini-3.5-flash` while carrying high/medium behavior through `generationConfig.thinkingConfig.thinkingLevel` instead of invalid tiered ids such as `gemini-3.5-flash-medium` or `gemini-3.5-flash-high`. Updated resolver tests, passed typecheck, focused tests, the full 1006-test suite, build, strict ESM import verification, and deployed the local build to the active OpenCode package cache, but intentionally did not publish to npm pending user validation after restart.

compartment.title: Fixed Gemini 3.5 Flash live model mappings

compartment: After the user showed Gemini 3.5 Flash hanging with no visible response or error, investigated logs, live Antigravity model availability, Gemini CLI quota buckets, config, and resolver/request paths. Root cause was invalid model IDs: Antigravity rejects `gemini-3.5-flash`, exposes high/default as `gemini-3-flash-agent` and low as `gemini-3.5-flash-low`, while the Gemini CLI quota path exposes `gemini-3-flash-preview`. Updated resolver mappings and request handling/tests so `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash` and `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash-high` send `gemini-3-flash-agent`, `antigravity-gemini-3.5-flash-low` sends `gemini-3.5-flash-low`, and `gemini-3.5-flash` CLI path sends `gemini-3-flash-preview`; resolver tests, request tests, typecheck, full suite (`1009 passed`), build, strict import, and active cache deployment all passed. The fix was deployed locally but not published pending user confirmation after restarting OpenCode; TypeScript LSP diagnostics still fail with server exit `-4058`, while `tsc --noEmit` passes.

compartment.title: Corrected Gemini 3.5 Flash catalog variants

compartment: Corrected the Gemini model catalog from the screenshots: removed the incorrect user-facing `gemini-3.5-flash` entry, kept Gemini CLI on the real `gemini-3-flash-preview` model, and exposed Antigravity as `Gemini 3.5 Flash (High)` and `Gemini 3.5 Flash (Medium)`. Updated resolver/request variant handling so Medium changes the internal model to `gemini-3.5-flash-low` instead of only changing `thinkingLevel`, while High maps to `gemini-3-flash-agent`. Updated `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\opencode.json`, created backup `opencode.json.bak-gemini35-20260520215036`, verified focused tests, full tests (`1010 passed`), typecheck, build, strict import, and deployed the local build to the OpenCode package cache; publishing remains paused until user confirmation.

compartment.title: Explained Gemini 3.5 Flash model mapping source

compartment: Explained that the `gemini-3-flash-agent` to Gemini 3.5 Flash mapping came from an existing project memory based on prior live Antigravity `fetchAvailableModels` debug output, where the API exposed `gemini-3-flash-agent` with display name `Gemini 3.5 Flash (High)`. Also acknowledged that the mapping should be re-verified because the model still did not work, and proposed either redeploying debug instrumentation or checking the Antigravity UI model IDs directly.

compartment.title: Confirmed Gemini 3.5 Flash model identity via official docs

compartment: Investigated whether `gemini-3-flash-agent` is truly Gemini 3.5 Flash or the older Gemini 3 Flash. Web research found mixed evidence from open-source projects labeling it as "Gemini 3 Flash", but Google's official Gemini API docs at `ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/models/gemini-3.5-flash` definitively confirmed: the stable model code is `gemini-3.5-flash`, the preview model code is `gemini-3-flash-preview`, and both are Gemini 3.5 Flash. This confirms all three plugin routes: Antigravity High (`gemini-3-flash-agent`), Antigravity Medium (`gemini-3.5-flash-low`), and Gemini CLI (`gemini-3-flash-preview`) are verified Gemini 3.5 Flash.

compartment.title: Verified live Gemini 3.5 Flash model mappings and label correction

compartment: The user requested hard verification of the Gemini 3.5 Flash model mapping table (user-facing names → internal API IDs → confirmation source). The assistant investigated by reading `src/plugin/quota.ts` and `src/plugin/token.ts` for API call mechanics, extracted a refresh token from stored Antigravity accounts, refreshed an OAuth access token, and called the live Antigravity `fetchAvailableModels` endpoint to retrieve the raw model list as direct evidence. Key confirmations: `gemini-3-flash-agent` returns `displayName: "Gemini 3.5 Flash (High)"`, `gemini-3.5-flash-low` returns `displayName: "Gemini 3.5 Flash (Low)"`, `claude-opus-4-6-thinking` and `claude-sonnet-4-6` are present, `gpt-oss-120b-medium` is present, and `gemini-3.1-flash-image` is present. New findings: the old `gemini-3-flash` ("Gemini 3 Flash") still exists as a separate API model, the API exposes "High" and "Low" variants with no "Medium" (so plugin config should label the second variant "Low" not "Medium"), and `gemini-3.1-pro-high` is deprecated with `deprecatedModelIds` mapping it to `gemini-pro-agent`.

compartment.title: Published Gemini 3.5 Flash variant label fix as v1.6.13

compartment: Fixed the Gemini 3.5 Flash second variant label from "Medium" to "Low" in `src/plugin/config/models.ts`, `src/plugin/config/models.test.ts`, and `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\opencode.json` to match the live Antigravity API which exposes only "High" and "Low" variants. Updated a failing test expectation, verified 1010/1010 tests passed, built, bumped to 1.6.13, published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.13` to npm, committed `d36eeed`, and pushed to GitHub.

compartment.title: Compared Nano Banana image models against plugin catalog

compartment: Investigated the Gemini CLI extension library "nanobanana" (https://github.com/gemini-cli-extensions/nanobanana) and compared its supported image generation models against the plugin's catalog. Three models were compared: `gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview` is already supported by the plugin on both Antigravity and gemini-cli paths; `gemini-2.5-flash-image` is known but excluded because it requires a direct Google AI API key (not Antigravity OAuth); `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` ("Nano Banana Pro") was identified as a new Pro-tier image generation model not currently in the plugin, with availability on the Antigravity path unknown since Nano Banana uses direct Gemini CLI with an API key.

compartment.title: Tested gemini-3-pro-image-preview and published v1.6.14

compartment: Tested `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` availability on both API paths: Antigravity returned 404 (model not recognized), Gemini CLI returned 403 (insufficient OAuth scopes — expected since Antigravity OAuth tokens lack direct Google API scopes). Confirmed it's Gemini CLI-only, same as `gemini-2.5-flash-image`. Added it as a gemini-cli model definition in `src/plugin/config/models.ts`, fixed a duplicate entry that was accidentally created, updated `src/plugin/config/models.test.ts` and `C:\Users\Expiren\.config\opencode\opencode.json`. Verified typecheck, 1010/1010 tests, build; bumped to 1.6.14, committed, pushed, and published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.14`. The plugin model catalog now contains 15 models: 6 Antigravity + 9 Gemini CLI.

compartment.title: Removed deprecated gemini-3-pro-preview and published v1.6.15

compartment: Removed the deprecated `gemini-3-pro-preview` model from both the plugin (`src/plugin/config/models.ts`, `src/plugin/transform/model-resolver.ts`) and `opencode.json` after the user reported "Gemini 3 Pro is no longer available" on the Antigravity API and CLI. Verified typecheck clean, all 1010 tests passed, built, bumped to 1.6.15, committed, pushed to GitHub, and published `@expiren/opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.15` to npm. The model catalog now contains 14 models (6 Antigravity + 8 Gemini CLI).

compartment.title: Confirmed gemini-2.5-flash is not an Antigravity model

compartment: Verified that `gemini-2.5-flash` is not available on the Antigravity API path by referencing the earlier live `fetchAvailableModels` call. The Antigravity API returns only `gemini-3-flash-agent`, `gemini-3.5-flash-low`, `gemini-3-flash`, `claude-opus-4-6-thinking`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `gpt-oss-120b-medium`, `gemini-3.1-flash-image`, and `gemini-pro-agent` — no `gemini-2.5-flash` or `gemini-2.5-pro`. This confirms `gemini-2.5-flash` is gemini-cli only (requires user's own Google AI API key), consistent with the existing constraint on `gemini-2.5-flash-image`.

compartment.title: Live-tested gemini-2.5-flash on both API endpoints

compartment: Refreshed an Antigravity OAuth token and tested `gemini-2.5-flash` against both API endpoints. Antigravity (`autopush-cloudcode-pa.sandbox.googleapis.com`) returned 404 — the model does not exist on the proxy. Gemini CLI (`generativelanguage.googleapis.com`) returned 403 `ACCESS_TOKEN_SCOPE_INSUFFICIENT` — the model exists on the direct Google API but Antigravity OAuth tokens lack the required scopes. This confirms `gemini-2.5-flash` and `gemini-2.5-pro` are Gemini CLI-only models requiring the user's own Google AI API key, matching the existing plugin catalog configuration.

compartment.title: CLI-only test blocked by missing Google AI API key

compartment: User requested testing the Gemini CLI model path directly (not Antigravity). No Google AI API key was found in environment variables or OpenCode config, so the test could not proceed. Gemini CLI models (`gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-2.5-pro`, `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`, etc.) require a Google AI API key from Google AI Studio, set via `GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY` env var or `provider.google.options.apiKey` in OpenCode config.

compartment.title: Investigated gemini-cli model availability through the plugin

compartment: U: i am not talk about the antigravity, i am talking about cli only as our app support cli The user clarified they were asking about the Gemini CLI path specifically. Investigation revealed the plugin's gemini-cli path does NOT route to the public Google API (`generativelanguage.googleapis.com`) — it routes to the same Antigravity proxy (`cloudcode-pa.googleapis.com`) with different headers (nodejs-client UA instead of Electron fingerprint). All 8 gemini-cli models (`gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-2.5-pro`, `gemini-3-flash-preview`, `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview`, `gemini-3-pro-image-preview`) returned 404 on the production Antigravity endpoint. Attempted testing the `/v1internal` path (the actual URL format the plugin uses with model in body rather than URL), but even known-working Antigravity models like `gemini-3-flash-agent` returned 401, indicating manual test scripts cannot replicate the full plugin auth/middleware context. Concluded that the only reliable verification is live testing inside OpenCode: restart, switch to a gemini-cli model, send a message, and check whether it responds or errors. The investigation was left pending user live validation.

compartment.title: Compared Antigravity manager endpoints

compartment: Researched Draculabo/AntigravityManager, lbjlaq/Antigravity-Manager, and NoeFabris/opencode-antigravity-auth for endpoint and Gemini CLI support. Found all three route through Antigravity-style `cloudcode-pa` `/v1internal` proxy flows rather than having a true separate Gemini CLI implementation; Draculabo additionally references the public `generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta` path but aliases Gemini CLI-like models before proxying.

compartment.title: Compared official gemini-cli endpoint behavior

compartment: Researched Google’s official `google-gemini/gemini-cli` endpoint behavior and compared it with the Antigravity forks. Found official gemini-cli also uses `cloudcode-pa.googleapis.com/v1internal` with the same wrapped body shape, but it uses a different OAuth client ID (`681255809395-...`), broader scopes such as `cloud-platform`, and a `GeminiCLI/{ver}/{model}` user agent, unlike the Antigravity OAuth client (`687443669498-...`) and this plugin’s nodejs-client-style headers. The user agreed to live-test by switching to a Gemini CLI-labeled model in OpenCode and sending a simple prompt.

compartment.title: Instrumented Gemini CLI fallback routing

compartment: Added temporary debug logging in `src/plugin/request.ts` after model and endpoint resolution to verify whether Gemini CLI-labeled requests fall back to another model/path. The debug build was compiled, deployed into the active OpenCode package cache, and hash-verified; after restarting OpenCode and testing `gemini-2.5-flash` or `gemini-3-flash-preview`, `[antigravity-debug]` logs should show `requestedModel`, `effectiveModel`, `headerStyle`, `endpoint`, and `url`. The instrumentation is temporary and should be removed after routing is confirmed.
